summary: Tested iter13: Daily Challenges (GET /challenges/today + POST /challenges/{id}/claim) and AR multi-spawn switching. 7 backend tests passed, 3 skipped (catch-driven progress and end-to-end claim — could not deterministically force a challenge to completion in the test window because catch attempts kept dodging). Frontend ChallengesCard pill + modal works (3 challenges, tier chips, progress text). AR other-spawns-strip works correctly: 4 thumbnails rendered, tapping switches active spawn (Otterpaw -> Mark verified).

minor.issue: _pick_daily_challenges uses Python's hash() which is randomized per process via PYTHONHASHSEED. Determinism only holds within a single backend process — restart the server and the same camper+date can get a DIFFERENT 3 challenges. Recommend using hashlib.sha1((camper_id+ymd).encode()).digest() to derive the seed for cross-process determinism.
Returns 200 with {ok, reward, balance, balances} — does not include new balances per ball type explicitly enough for FE auto-refresh; FE workaround is to call /wallet again. Acceptable but minor.

issues: The 'Add to home screen' onboarding modal overlays the challenges-pill on first session and there is no auto-set localStorage flag once dismissed for the rrdc_onboarded_{userId} key tied to the home-screen modal (different key pathway). Test had to set the flag manually. UX-wise the user would naturally dismiss it but worth confirming the flag is persisted.

action_items: Replace hash() with a stable deterministic seed (e.g. hashlib.sha1) in _pick_daily_challenges so picks survive backend restarts.
Optional: surface 'completed' fast for the FE: the frontend ChallengesCard polls every 30s; consider returning new {challenges} embedded in the claim response for instant refresh.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _pick_daily_challenges uses non-deterministic hash() — per-camper seed will change on every backend restart (PYTHONHASHSEED is randomized).

_challenge_progress for 'distinct_types' uses db.catches.distinct on 'pokemon_type' — make sure catches are created with that field; otherwise 'two_types'/'three_types' challenges silently never advance.

/challenges/today does not error when the picked template list contains duplicate 'kind' (e.g. catch_total appears in easy & hard tiers, RNG can pick both, leaving only one tier of progress visible and the second redundant). Consider deduping by kind.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (pill, modal, 3 items, tier chips, progress text, AR strip switching)

test_credentials: admin/Camp1993 for admin; campers via GET /api/groups/{code}/campers + POST /api/camper/login. Used B02 group, Ruben Blackston for FE; B01 Hudson for BE progress checks.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded campers and pokemon.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter13 progress tests skip because catch RNG keeps dodging. To reproduce claim e2e: log in a B01 camper with 'catch_3_today' easy challenge and run /api/spawn/catch in a loop until 3 catches succeed (caught=true), then POST /api/challenges/catch_3_today/claim. The other-spawns-strip on /ar requires lat/lng query (mock 40.6396,-73.6665) so /spawn/current returns >=2 spawns. Use localStorage rrdc_onboarded_{userId}='1' before navigating to /map to skip onboarding overlay during automated tests.
rca of the issue: Skipped backend tests are not bugs — they are environmental: catch_total/throw_count progress requires either (a) the camper's 3 picks to include that kind AND (b) at least one /spawn/catch attempt to succeed. With current spawn rates, the test loop dodged in all attempts. Underlying counters/queries verified via _challenge_progress code review and via incomplete-claim 400 response which echoes 'Not complete yet (X/N)' with the live progress.
